diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index ae0dd68..788c3fb 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -156,2 +156,8 @@ Week 24 / day 161 observations:
 - Week 24 action: switch capacity tier 3 -> 2 and C model tier 4 -> 3 for one survival week. Keep prices, A/B/C quotas (C remains 500k), no ads/dev, and lean S2 ops.
 - This may risk downgrades/quality perception and overload, but cash survival requires cutting weekly burn. Repeat $9 E3 offers only.
+
+Week 25 / day 168 observations:
+- C tier 3 was a mistake: cash fell to $11.7k, cancellations spiked to 262, downgrades 94, and C MRR dropped sharply. Tier 2 capacity itself was fine with no overload/outage.
+- Restore C model tier 4 immediately while keeping capacity tier 2. This is the only viable compromise: restore product quality without returning to tier 3 capacity cost.
+- Next billing window is about $43k, enough to try C tier 4 + tier 2 + lean ops. Keep C quota at 500k, prices unchanged, ads/dev off, and S2 targeted ops unchanged.
+- Repeat $9 E3 offers, though enterprise remains effectively non-converting.
